Title: IbPIF1 confers stem nematode resistance by regulating secondary metabolites in sweet potato

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Terpenoid biosynthesis via the mevalonate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates mevalonate 5-diphosphate to form isopentenyl diphosphate in the mevalonate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Terpenoids (especially sesquiterpenes, including curcumenol-associated defence metabolites)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defensive secondary metabolites that inhibit stem nematode activity and contribute to plant resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in storage roots after stem nematode infection; higher in IbPIF1-OE and IbMVD-OE lines than WT
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in sweet potato; RT-qPCR; stem nematode inoculation assay; histological analysis; metabolite profiling of sesquiterpenes/terpenoids; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbMVD acts downstream of IbPIF1 and is directly activated by IbPIF1 through promoter binding. 2. Overexpression of IbMVD enhances resistance to stem nematode infection in sweet potato. 3. IbMVD overexpression increases expression of terpenoid- and defence-related genes and elevates sesquiterpene, lignin and callose accumulation after infection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes terpenoid/sesquiterpene accumulation and enhances stem nematode resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a pivotal downstream gene in terpenoid synthesis regulation in this study; classical pathway rate-limiting status not established.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to enhance terpenoid-mediated nematode resistance in sweet potato; may be valuable alone or stacked with upstream regulators such as IbPIF1.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth or morphological penalty reported for overexpression lines in the study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: IbMVD promoter contains a G-box motif bound by IbPIF1; IbMVD overexpression also increased lignin and callose, suggesting broader defence-network effects beyond precursor supply.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor with bHLH, APA and APB domains; PIF family
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Terpenoids; lignin; callose; methyl jasmonate-associated defence metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Terpenoids function as defensive metabolites inhibiting stem nematode activity; lignin and callose reinforce barriers; JA contributes to systemic defence.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of secondary metabolite biosynthesis and defence responses, especially terpenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates IbMVD by binding the G-box-containing promoter; also positively associated with higher expression of IbFPS; IbTPS; IbGDS; IbLOX; IbAOS; IbOPR3; IbPAL; IbHRGP; IbCPI; IbSOD; IbCAT; IbAPX
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced after stem nematode infection; higher expression in resistant cultivar Lushu 3 than susceptible Lizixiang
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in transgenic overexpression lines and induced after infection in storage roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and RNAi in sweet potato; disease resistance assay; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; metabolomics; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; subcellular localization; yeast transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbPIF1 is a nuclear bHLH transcriptional activator induced by stem nematode infection and more highly expressed in the resistant cultivar. 2. IbPIF1 overexpression enhances, while RNAi reduces, resistance to stem nematode in sweet potato storage roots. 3. IbPIF1 promotes accumulation of defensive metabolites and structures, including terpenoids, lignin, callose, and JA-related responses. 4. IbPIF1 directly binds and activates the IbMVD promoter, linking transcriptional regulation to terpenoid-mediated defence.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances stem nematode resistance by increasing terpenoid accumulation and associated defence responses.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pivotal upstream regulator controlling terpenoid-mediated resistance through direct activation of IbMVD and broader defence gene induction.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value regulatory target for breeding or engineering stem nematode-resistant sweet potato by boosting defensive secondary metabolites; could be stacked with IbMVD for stronger terpenoid-based resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant growth or morphological differences reported in overexpression or RNAi plants under the conditions tested.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcriptional activation domain mapped to the N-terminal 200 aa; direct promoter binding to IbMVD was validated in yeast, sweet potato protoplasts, and in vitro.
